# 🧪 clerkR

**A clerk keeps tabs — `clerkR` keeps yours publication-ready.**

[![License:
MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](https://clerkr.circadia-lab.uk/LICENSE)
[![R](https://img.shields.io/badge/R-%3E%3D4.1.0-276DC3)](https://cran.r-project.org/)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 📖 What is clerkR?

`clerkR` transforms standard R data frames into publication-ready tables
for biomedical and neuroscience manuscripts. Rather than wrestling with
formatting each time, `clerkR` reduces the most common table types to a
handful of opinionated constructor functions that share consistent
theming, domain grouping, footnote handling, and a unified rendering
pipeline for both Word/PDF (`gt`) and interactive HTML (`reactable`)
output.

## ✨ Features

- 📋 **Five table archetypes** covering ~90% of what appears in a biomed
  paper
- 🗂️ **Domain/section grouping** — organise rows under labelled headers
- 📝 **Automatic footnotes** for log-transformed variables and FDR
  correction
- 🎨 **Dual render targets** —
  [`render_gt()`](https://clerkr.circadia-lab.uk/reference/render_gt.md)
  for Word/PDF,
  [`render_reactable()`](https://clerkr.circadia-lab.uk/reference/render_reactable.md)
  for HTML
- 🔢 **Significance annotation** — p-values, FDR-corrected p, stars
  (toggleable)
- 🧩 **Composable** — all constructors return a `clerk_tbl` S3 object

## 🗂️ Project Structure

    clerkR/
    ├── R/
    │   ├── clerkR-package.R       # Package-level documentation
    │   ├── tbl_descriptive.R      # Archetype 1: Table 1 / descriptive
    │   ├── render.R               # render_gt() and render_reactable()
    │   └── clerk_example.R        # Synthetic example dataset docs
    ├── data/
    │   └── clerk_example.rda      # Synthetic example dataset
    ├── data-raw/
    │   └── clerk_example.R        # Script to generate example data
    ├── tests/testthat/
    │   └── test-tbl_descriptive.R
    ├── man/                       # Auto-generated by roxygen2
    ├── DESCRIPTION
    ├── NAMESPACE
    └── README.md

## 🚀 Getting Started

### Prerequisites

``` r

install.packages(c("dplyr", "tidyr", "rlang", "gt", "reactable"))
```

### Installation

``` r

# Development version from GitHub
remotes::install_github("circadia-bio/clerkR")
```

### Basic usage

``` r

library(clerkR)

# Descriptive / Table 1
tbl_descriptive(
  clerk_example,
  group   = sex,
  domains = list(
    "Metabolic"     = c("hdl", "glucose", "bmi"),
    "Anthropometric"= c("waist", "systolic_bp"),
    "Cognitive"     = c("tmt_time", "verbal_fluency"),
    "Mental health" = c("bdi", "panas_neg", "life_satisfaction")
  ),
  log_vars = c("tmt_time"),
  fdr      = TRUE
) |>
  render_gt(title = "Table 1. Sample characteristics by sex")
```

## 📦 Dependencies

| Package     | Version  | Purpose                    |
|-------------|----------|----------------------------|
| `dplyr`     | ≥ 1.1.0  | Data manipulation          |
| `tidyr`     | any      | Reshaping                  |
| `rlang`     | any      | Tidy evaluation            |
| `gt`        | ≥ 0.10.0 | Word/PDF table rendering   |
| `reactable` | ≥ 0.4.0  | Interactive HTML rendering |

## 👥 Authors

| Role               | Name                                           |
|--------------------|------------------------------------------------|
| Author, maintainer | [Lucas G. S. França](https://github.com/lgsfr) |

## 🤝 Related Tools

- 🕐 [**zeitR**](https://github.com/circadia-bio/zeitR) — actigraphy
  analysis and circadian metrics
- 📓 [**slumbR**](https://github.com/circadia-bio/slumbR) — sleep diary
  processing
- 📋 [**tallieR**](https://github.com/circadia-bio/tallieR) —
  questionnaire and sociodemographic data
- 🔗 [**syncR**](https://github.com/circadia-bio/syncR) — integrates
  zeitR, slumbR, and tallieR into a unified database
- 🔬 [**circadia-bio**](https://github.com/circadia-bio) — the Circadia
  Lab GitHub organisation

License](https://clerkr.circadia-lab.uk/LICENSE).

# Package index

## Table constructors

Build publication-ready tables from data frames.

- [`tbl_descriptive()`](https://clerkr.circadia-lab.uk/reference/tbl_descriptive.md)
  : Descriptive summary table with group comparisons (Table 1)

## Render

Render a clerk_tbl to gt, HTML, or LaTeX.

- [`render()`](https://clerkr.circadia-lab.uk/reference/render.md) :
  Render a clerk_tbl to its target output format
- [`render_gt()`](https://clerkr.circadia-lab.uk/reference/render_gt.md)
  : Render a clerk_tbl as a gt table (Word / PDF)
- [`render_reactable()`](https://clerkr.circadia-lab.uk/reference/render_reactable.md)
  : Render a clerk_tbl as an interactive HTML table
- [`render_latex()`](https://clerkr.circadia-lab.uk/reference/render_latex.md)
  : Render a clerk_tbl as a LaTeX table

## Theme & palette

clerkR visual identity — colours, scales, and gt theme.

- [`clerk_theme()`](https://clerkr.circadia-lab.uk/reference/clerk_theme.md)
  : Apply the clerkR gt theme to a gt table
- [`clerk_palette()`](https://clerkr.circadia-lab.uk/reference/clerk_palette.md)
  : clerkR colour palette
- [`clerk_colour()`](https://clerkr.circadia-lab.uk/reference/clerk_colour.md)
  : Access a single clerkR colour by name
- [`clerk_diverging()`](https://clerkr.circadia-lab.uk/reference/clerk_diverging.md)
  : Build a clerkR diverging colour scale
- [`clerk_sequential()`](https://clerkr.circadia-lab.uk/reference/clerk_sequential.md)
  : Build a clerkR sequential colour scale

## Data

Synthetic example dataset.

- [`clerk_example`](https://clerkr.circadia-lab.uk/reference/clerk_example.md)
  : Synthetic example dataset for clerkR

## Package

Package-level documentation.

- [`clerkR`](https://clerkr.circadia-lab.uk/reference/clerkR-package.md)
  [`clerkR-package`](https://clerkr.circadia-lab.uk/reference/clerkR-package.md)
  : clerkR: Publication-Ready Tables for Biomedical Research
